mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as long as rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.72% as of t-1mo suggests room for easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of t-1mo, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with recession tail risk
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness and negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100k while UNRATE stable within 0.2pp
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated signals demand destruction in interest-rate-sensitive sectors combined with persistent cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as sector rotation and margin compression occur.
regime_hint: stagflation or demand-supply imbalance with rising rates
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines while federal funds rate remains elevated signals monetary policy tightening cycle nearing completion, reducing real discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as terminal rate expectations stabilize.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate signals economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand and valuations compress.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
